content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 2,
1994
Frances Liston
City Manager/City Clerk
Town of Colma
1198 El Camino Real
Colma,
CA
94014
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—94-061
Dear Ms.
Liston:
This is in response to your letter requesting confirmation of
telephone advice provided to you on behalf of Colma City
Councilmember Charles Gerrans concerning his duties under the
conflict-of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”).
1
This letter confirms the advice provided to you and
Councilmember Gerrans on February 24,
1994.
You stated that the city council will be considering
applications to manage a cardrooxn that was previously approved for
the jurisdiction by the voters.
One of the applicants has
proposed a parking 1t on property owned by Salem Cemetery.
Councilmember Gerrans resides in a home on Salem Cemetery property
located 1,000 feet from the proposed parking lot.
You stated that
the councilmember has resided at that site since
1963
(prior to
being elected to the city council)
and currently pays
$225 per
month to rent the premises on a month—to—month basis.
The
councilmember pays for his
own utilities,
and repairs and
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18954.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 800 •
P.O. Box 807 •
Sacramento, CA
95804-0807 •
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-94-06l
Page
2
maintenance on the house.
You did not indicate that the
councilmember had any interest in any of the applicants.
As we discussed,
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any
public official at any level of state or local government from
making, participating in making,
or in any way attempting to use
his or her official position to influence a governmental decision
which will have a reasonably foreseeable and material financial
effect on the official’s economic interest.
Section 87103 provides that the councilmember has a financial
interest in any decision which will have a reasonably foreseeable
material financial effect on:
(1)
Councilmember Gerrans or a member of his immediate
family;
(2)
Any business entity in which Councilmember Gerrans has
an investment worth $1,000 or more or holds a position as
a
director, officer, partner,
trustee,
employee,
or manager
(Sections 87103(a)
and
(d));
(3)
Any real property in which Councilmember Gerrans has an
interest worth $1,000 or more
(Section 87103(b));
or,
(4)
Any source of income or gifts of
$250 or more to
Councilmember Gerrans within
12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(Sections 87103(c)
and
(e).)
The definition of
“income” includes loans and forgiveness of indebtedness.
(Section
82030.)
Section 82028 defines “gift” to include a rebate or
discount in the price of anything of value unless the rebate or
discount is made in the regular course of business to members of
the public without regard to official status.
As I advised,
since the councilmember rents the home on a
month—to—month basis,
he does not have an interest in the real
property on which the house is situated.
(Section 82033;
Regulation 18233.)
Moreover,
according to your facts,
the councilmember is not
an employee of Salem Cemetery,
nor is the cemetery a source of
income or gifts to the councilinember.
(Sections 87103(c),
(d),
and (e).)
You stated that the councilmember pays the fair market
value for the cemetery home.
2
Finally,
you stated that the councilmember’s rent would not
be affected by the decision.
Thus,
as I advised,
Councilmember
2
You stated that Colma had several cemeteries and that the rent
the councilmember pays is the equivalent to what is paid for other
cemetery houses.


Our File No.
A-94--061
Page
3
Gerrans would not have a conflict of interest in the decision.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322-59O1.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
G neral Counsel
By:
ohn
ounsel,
Legal Division
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at
a
cost of
lOc per page.

sections.question: Whether Councilmember Charles Gerrans of the Town of Colma has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act in voting on applications to manage a cardroom, where one applicant has proposed a parking lot on property owned by Salem Cemetery, and the councilmember resides in a home on Salem Cemetery property located 1,000 feet from the proposed parking lot.
sections.conclusion: Councilmember Gerrans would not have a conflict of interest in the decision. Since the councilmember rents the home on a month-to-month basis, he does not have an interest in the real property. The councilmember is not an employee of Salem Cemetery, nor is the cemetery a source of income or gifts. The councilmember pays fair market value for the cemetery home, and his rent would not be affected by the decision.
sections.question_synthetic: Does Councilmember Gerrans have a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act when voting on cardroom management applications where one applicant proposes a parking lot on Salem Cemetery property, given that the councilmember rents a home on the same cemetery property?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that Councilmember Gerrans has no conflict of interest because he only rents the home on a month-to-month basis (so lacks real property interest), is not employed by or receiving income/gifts from Salem Cemetery, pays fair market rent, and the decision will not affect his rental terms.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is an FPPC response letter confirming prior telephone advice. Contains clear Question (implicit in facts) and Conclusion sections. The advice addresses conflict of interest analysis under Government Code Sections 87100 and 87103.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether Councilmember Charles Gerrans of the Town of Colma has a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act in voting on applications to manage a cardroom, where one applicant has proposed a parking lot on property owned by Salem Cemetery, and the councilmember resides in a home on Salem Cemetery property located 1,000 feet from the proposed parking lot.

CONCLUSION: Councilmember Gerrans would not have a conflict of interest in the decision. Since the councilmember rents the home on a month-to-month basis, he does not have an interest in the real property. The councilmember is not an employee of Salem Cemetery, nor is the cemetery a source of income or gifts. The councilmember pays fair market value for the cemetery home, and his rent would not be affected by the decision.

embedding.summary: This FPPC advice letter confirms telephone advice given to the Town of Colma regarding whether Councilmember Charles Gerrans has a conflict of interest in voting on cardroom management applications where one applicant proposes a parking lot on Salem Cemetery property where the councilmember resides. The FPPC concluded no conflict exists because the councilmember lacks a qualifying financial interest.
